description: Recto and verso (secondary use): Accounts in the hand of Nahray b. Nissim. Dating: ca. 1053 (Gil). Listing flax purchases. Nahray organized the list by grouping transactions with specific business partners who had ordered flax. He notes down how much each partner paid individual flax growers out of that partner’s money. Among flax growers are the qāḍī and the preacher of a village, as well as some Copts. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 2, p. 912.) Also mentions the jahbadh (money-changer, tax-cashier, or, in older scholarship following Fischel, government banker), as well as people who are probably Jews, such as Yeshu'a and Barhūm. The lists also mention Busīrī flax and money to be exchanged in the Fayyūm. (Information from Goitein’s index card.)
tags: trade, Nahray B. Nissim, account, Christians, flax
description_he: חשבונות בכתב יד נהריי בן נסים, בערך 1053. רשימת רכישות פשתן, מקובצת לפי שותפים עסקיים.
